sections.paragraph:
πρόοιδα (Hdt. et al.; Epict. 2, 10, 5; PSI 349, 8 [III b.c.]; Wsd 19:1; 4 Macc 4:25; Just., D. 103, 4; Ath. 21, 5) perf. (w. pres. mng.), ptc. προειδώς; plpf. προῇδειν know beforehand/previously w. gen. (Menand., Peric. 472 S. [222 Kö.]; Polyb. 5, 13, 5 τὸ μέλλον; Herodian 7, 1, 9) Ac 2:31 v.l.; IPhld 7:2 v.l. (s. Bihlmeyer ad loc.). Foll. by acc. and inf. 1 Cl 43:6.

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

perf. perf. = perfect

pres. pres. = present

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

ptc. ptc. = participle

plpf. plpf. = pluperfect

gen. gen. = genitive

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

Bihlmeyer Bihlmeyer = KB., Die Apostolischen Väter—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

acc. acc. = accusative

inf. inf. = infinitive

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 873
